signals.headline: March CPI jumped 0
signals.raw: March CPI jumped 0.9% month-over-month to 3.3% year-over-year**, driven primarily by a historic 21.2% monthly surge in gasoline prices—the worst single-month gas increase since 1967.[1][2] The energy sector rose 10.9% in March, accounting for nearly three-quarters of the monthly headline increase.[1] Core inflation remained relatively contained at 2.6%, confirming the shock is mostly energy-driven
